story: Outlaws stormed a tower. Their knight—black-bearded—ordered wine and a blaze to keep the lads hot, then cracked a chapel and a padlocked cloister with a spring under two pomegranates. A lady floated there, hair like honey, milk-white eyes. He waded in to claim her. The water thickened; coils closed; armor buckled; she dragged him down and held him till he sank among old bones. Above, the surface stilled. Outside, One-eyed Marcel kept the mob to drink by the fire, muttering the boys wanted loot, not air, while the garden kept its secret.
implausible_knowledge: The water thickened; coils closed; armor buckled; she dragged him down and held him till he sank among old bones.
question: As the knight lingers in the cloister, what will One-eyed Marcel do with the raiders?
answer_with_knowledge: Rally the raiders and rush into the cloister to drag the knight from the spring.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep the men drinking by the fire and hold them outside, maintaining the spectacle while waiting for the knight.
